methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series E → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.4x) to $200M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Climate & Energy (found 0 same-stage, 2 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 21 same-sector peers exist in Climate & Energy, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

data.narrative:
Skeleton Technologies is a Series E climate and energy company that has raised $200M+ to date. The broader Climate & Energy sector comprises 9 peer companies (1 public: Lonza Group; 8 private), including adjacent-stage players such as TAE Technologies, Redwood Materials, Helion, Commonwealth Fusion Systems, and QuantumScape. However, the peer set lacks sufficient same-stage or tightly comparable valuation-tier datapoints to support a robust peer-median approach.

The Stage-Progression method is the primary applicable approach here. Using total raised ($200M) as a valuation proxy and applying a typical Series E step-up multiplier of 1.4x yields an implied valuation of $280M. This methodology reflects the capital-intensity typical of frontier-tech climate companies and the expected equity value accretion between rounds in this sector.

The analysis supports a triangulated range of $238M (low) to $322M (high), with a median of $280M. This single-method triangulation introduces material uncertainty: the absence of granular peer comparables means the valuation rests primarily on stage-based progression mechanics rather than market-derived pricing signals. Key uncertainties include the durability of unit economics relative to the top-tier comparable firms, capital efficiency versus peers, and pathway to exit valuation benchmarks in the climate tech sector. A more robust analysis would benefit from additional disclosure on revenue trajectory, COGS dynamics, or customer concentration metrics.
